reasoning: The probability of substantive AI regulation passing by January 1, 2026, is negligible. As of late October 2025, no comprehensive AI regulatory bills have passed. The Trump administration has taken a strongly deregulatory stance, rescinding Biden-era AI policies and issuing Executive Order 14179 to "Remove Barriers" to AI leadership. An attempt to pass a moratorium on state AI laws via the budget reconciliation package was stripped out by Congress. The only major AI-related legislation moving is the GAIN AI Act attached to the Senate's NDAA, but it strictly imposes hardware export/trade controls (requiring chipmakers to prioritize U.S. buyers) and does not set performance, safety, or capability thresholds for AI models/systems themselves. Even then, the White House opposes it. With only two months left in the year, a divided Congress, and an explicitly anti-regulatory executive branch, there is neither the time nor the political will to enact legislation meeting the criteria. I am keeping the forecast at the minimum possible given the constraints, closely aligning with the crowd forecast of 1%.
